findings.issue: Directory creation race: Task 2 creates skills/design-system-init/references/font-pairing.md (implicitly creating skills/design-system-init/references/). Task 3 creates skills/design-system-init/SKILL.md and states 'Директория уже существует после Task 2'. This assumption is correct IF Task 2 creates the parent dir skills/design-system-init/ as a side effect of creating the references/ subdir. Both tasks are in different waves (T2=Wave1, T3=Wave2), so the dependency is declared (T3 depends_on: [1,2]). The dependency chain is correct. However — Task 3 Details states 'Директория уже существует после Task 2 (font-pairing.md создаётся в skills/design-system-init/references/)' — this is true, but it's an implicit side-effect dependency. If Task 2 is ever skipped or re-ordered, Task 3 will not create the parent directory itself. Task 3's 'What to do' step 1 says 'Создать директорию skills/design-system-init/ (если ещё не создана задачей 2)' — the guard is present but the responsibility is ambiguous. This is a minor ambiguity, not a blocking issue. Reclassifying to major.
findings.fix: Reclassified to major — see finding below.

findings.issue: Task 3 implicitly relies on Task 2 to create the skills/design-system-init/ parent directory as a side effect. Task 3 step 1 says 'create if not already created by Task 2'. This creates an ambiguous ownership of directory initialization — if Task 2 is executed independently or re-run, the parent directory state is unpredictable. Tech-spec does not designate an explicit owner for creating skills/design-system-init/ (only for shared/design-references/ which is owned by Task 1).
findings.fix: Task 3 should unconditionally create skills/design-system-init/ in its own step 1, removing the '(если ещё не создана задачей 2)' qualifier. Task 2 creating a subdirectory is sufficient for its own scope — Task 3 should own its root directory creation explicitly.

findings.issue: Task 7 references do-task Step 2.3 and Step 2.4 by number, stating 'Пункт 3 (строка 48): Git commit' and 'Пункт 4 (строка 49): For each reviewer'. The actual do-task SKILL.md has step 3 at line 48 (git commit) and step 4 at line 49 (reviewers) — this matches. However, Task 7 says the new hook should be inserted as a new step between items 3 and 4, making reviewer become step 5. The current do-task Step 2 has exactly 4 numbered items (1=load skills, 2=follow workflow, 3=git commit, 4=reviewers). After insertion, item 4 becomes item 5. Task 7 accepts this renumbering in edge cases. This is fine structurally. BUT: Task 7 depends_on: [5] only — it does NOT declare dependency on Task 3, 4, or 6. The task reads design-review/SKILL.md (created by Task 5) to understand how to reference design-review when calling it as a subagent. This is correct. No critical issue here — reclassifying.
findings.fix: Reclassified — not critical. See finding below.

findings.issue: Task 7 depends_on: [5] (design-review skill) but does NOT declare depends_on on Task 3 (design-system-init), Task 4 (design-generate), or Task 6 (design-retrospective). While Task 7 only modifies do-task and only needs design-review/SKILL.md to exist, it is in Wave 3 alongside Task 8. Task 8 depends_on: [1,2,3,4,5,6] — which is correct and comprehensive. Task 7 should ideally also not be executed until the design-review skill is fully validated, but it only strictly needs Task 5. The dependency declaration is technically sufficient but inconsistent with Task 8's conservative approach.
findings.fix: Minor inconsistency only — Task 7's depends_on: [5] is logically sufficient since it only reads design-review/SKILL.md. No change required, but note the inconsistency with Task 8's more defensive depends_on pattern.

findings.issue: Task 2 and Task 8 reference project-knowledge files at path shared/templates/new-project/.agents/skills/project-knowledge/references/project.md and architecture.md in their Context Files section. These files exist at C:/tmp/ai-dev-repo/shared/templates/new-project/.agents/skills/project-knowledge/references/ (verified: project.md, architecture.md, deployment.md, patterns.md, ux-guidelines.md are present). The paths in task context files use relative paths from the feature folder: '../../../shared/templates/new-project/...' — this is the correct relative path from work/design-pipeline/tasks/. No issue with existence. However, the prompt states 'This repo has no project-knowledge — skip PK-related findings'. Marking as informational only.
findings.fix: N/A — per validator instructions, PK-related findings are skipped.

findings.issue: Task 4 reads grid-techniques.md from work/design-pipeline/references/grid-techniques.md (confirmed to exist). Task 4 also copies it to skills/design-generate/references/grid-techniques.md. However, Task 4's Context Files section references grid-techniques.md with path '../references/grid-techniques.md' — this is relative to work/design-pipeline/tasks/, which resolves to work/design-pipeline/references/grid-techniques.md. This is correct. BUT: shared/design-references/ does NOT include grid-techniques.md per tech-spec (it is design-generate-specific). Task 4 also reads 'design-tokens.md' from '../../shared/design-references/design-tokens.md' — but shared/design-references/ does not exist yet (it is created by Task 1, which is in Wave 1, and Task 4 is in Wave 2). Task 4 declares depends_on: [1], so at runtime shared/design-references/design-tokens.md will exist. Dependency is correctly declared. No issue.
findings.fix: No fix required — dependency is correctly declared.

findings.issue: Tasks 3, 4, 5, 6 are all in Wave 2 and will be executed in parallel by the agent framework. Each creates a different top-level skill directory: skills/design-system-init/ (T3), skills/design-generate/ (T4), skills/design-review/ (T5), skills/design-retrospective/ (T6). These directories are completely separate — no overlap exists. Task 2 (Wave 1) pre-creates skills/design-system-init/references/ as a side effect. Tasks 4, 5, 6 each create their own fresh directories. No wave conflict or race condition possible since all writes are to distinct directories. This is clean.
findings.fix: No fix required — Wave 2 tasks write to non-overlapping directories.

findings.issue: Task 7 implementation hints say 'Формат вызова субагента: аналогичен вызову reviewer (spawn subagent), но с agent_type = design-review'. The existing do-task Step 4 spawns reviewers using spawn_agent with agent_type = reviewer name. design-review is being called as agent_type='design-review'. However, the existing design-review skill does not exist yet in the codebase (it will be created by Task 5) — this is fine since Task 7 depends_on Task 5. The actual agent_type value for design-review needs to match whatever the skill system uses to identify skills. The hint is consistent with how do-task already calls reviewers. Acceptable.
findings.fix: No change required — pattern is consistent with existing reviewer invocation pattern.

findings.issue: Task 4 copies grid-techniques.md from work/design-pipeline/references/grid-techniques.md to skills/design-generate/references/grid-techniques.md. Task 1 copies color-psychology.md and color-principles.md from work/design-pipeline/references/ to shared/design-references/. Both tasks handle the work/design-pipeline/references/ source files, but they copy to different destinations and copy different files — no overlap. Task 1 does NOT delete the originals (edge cases explicitly say 'Оригинальные файлы НЕ удалять в этой задаче'). Task 8 deletes like-figma but not work/design-pipeline/references/. No task is designated to clean up work/design-pipeline/references/ after migration. The source files will remain permanently unless manually removed. This is a minor gap — not a functional problem.
findings.fix: Consider adding a cleanup step to Task 8 or Task 12 to remove work/design-pipeline/references/ after migration is verified, or document in decisions.md that these source files are intentionally retained.

findings.issue: Tasks 9, 10, 11 (Audit Wave) all declare depends_on: [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8] and are in Wave 4. They all read the same set of skill files (design-system-init, design-generate, design-review, design-retrospective SKILL.md files) concurrently. Since all three are read-only audit tasks writing to different output files (task-9/code-audit-report.json, task-10/security-audit.md, task-11/test-audit-report.md), there is no write conflict. However, if any audit task (e.g., Task 9) identifies a critical issue requiring a fix, Task 10 and Task 11 will be auditing the unfixed version in parallel. This could lead to redundant findings across audit tasks with no remediation cycle built in. Task 12 (QA) depends_on: [9,10,11] and is the remediation gate.
findings.fix: Acceptable design: audit tasks are parallel reads, Task 12 is the consolidation gate. If audits find critical issues, Task 12 will catch them. No structural change needed.

findings.issue: Task 8's smoke verification step 4 runs: grep -r 'like-figma' skills/ shared/ --include='*.md' and filters out decisions.md, tech-spec.md, user-spec.md, and tasks/. However, the task files themselves (work/design-pipeline/tasks/1.md through 12.md) contain multiple references to 'like-figma' as historical context. The grep command explicitly excludes 'tasks/' from the filter: '| grep -v tasks/' — this means the task files will be searched but filtered from output. The grep searches 'skills/ shared/' not 'work/', so task files at work/design-pipeline/tasks/ won't even be in scope. Correct — no issue.
findings.fix: No fix required — grep scope is skills/ and shared/ only, not work/.

findings.issue: Task 4 copies grid-techniques.md from work/design-pipeline/references/grid-techniques.md. Task 8 deletes skills/like-figma/ and verifies migration. Task 8 step 3 verifies 'skills/design-generate/references/grid-techniques.md' exists — correct. But Task 8 does NOT verify that the original work/design-pipeline/references/ source files were the correct content. More importantly: Task 4 is in Wave 2 and Task 8 is in Wave 3. Task 8 depends_on includes Task 4. This is correct. No critical issue — reclassifying.

Task 6 (design-retrospective) declares depends_on: [1] (Wave 1 shared references). In the Details section it explicitly states 'Фактически design-retrospective автономен — он работает с проектными файлами .design-system/, а не с reference-файлами скиллов.' If the skill truly does not reference any files from shared/design-references/, the depends_on: [1] dependency is unnecessary/misleading. It implies a wave ordering dependency that doesn't reflect actual file dependencies.
findings.fix: If design-retrospective SKILL.md does not link to any files from shared/design-references/, remove depends_on: [1] and change to depends_on: [] to accurately reflect the true independence of the task. Alternatively, document in Details that the depends_on is a wave-ordering convention, not a file dependency.

findings.issue: Task 4's skill-master path in Required Skills uses a relative path '../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' (two levels up from work/design-pipeline/tasks/). All other Wave 2 tasks (T3, T5, T6) use '../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' (three levels up). The correct path from work/design-pipeline/tasks/ to skills/ is indeed three levels up (tasks/ -> design-pipeline/ -> work/ -> repo root -> skills/). Task 4's path is wrong — it resolves to work/skills/skill-master/SKILL.md which does not exist.
findings.fix: Task 4 Required Skills section: change '[skills/skill-master/SKILL.md](../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md)' to '[skills/skill-master/SKILL.md](../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md)'. Same fix needed in Context Files section: 'skill-master/SKILL.md' currently uses '../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' — change to '../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md'.

findings.issue: Task 2 Required Skills section references skill-master at '~/.claude/skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' using a home-directory path. All other tasks use relative paths from the repo root (e.g., '../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md'). The tilde path '~/.claude/skills/' is a different installation location from the project's 'skills/' directory. If the project's skill-master is in C:/tmp/ai-dev-repo/skills/skill-master/, the ~/ path may resolve to a different (potentially older) version.
findings.fix: Task 2 Required Skills: change '[skills/skill-master/SKILL.md](~/.claude/skills/skill-master/SKILL.md)' to use the consistent relative path '../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' matching all other tasks. Same fix for the like-figma context file reference which also appears to use a full path.

findings.issue: Task 8 uses skills: [code-writing] while all other Wave 1-3 tasks use skills: [skill-master]. Task 8 is a deletion task with no skill content creation — code-writing is the appropriate skill. But code-writing is listed in the actual skills/ directory (verified). The code-writing skill path '~/.claude/skills/code-writing/SKILL.md' in Required Skills uses the tilde path like Task 2, not the repo-relative path. Consistent with Task 2's issue.
findings.fix: Task 8 Required Skills: change '[SKILL.md](~/.claude/skills/code-writing/SKILL.md)' to '../../../skills/code-writing/SKILL.md' for consistency with other tasks.

wave_4_audit.conflicts: none — distinct output files, all reads shared

shared_design_references.status: correct — all consumers are in Wave 2+ and declare depends_on: [1]

like_figma_source.status: correct — deletion only after all consumers complete

consumers: Task 8 reads to verify, Task 9 audits, Task 12 QA
do_task_skill_md.status: correct — Task 8,9,12 all have Task 7 in their dependency chain

actionable_findings_summary.action: Fix relative path in Required Skills: '../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md' → '../../../skills/skill-master/SKILL.md'. Same fix for Context Files skill-master reference.
Fix code-writing skill path from '~/.claude/skills/code-writing/SKILL.md' to '../../../skills/code-writing/SKILL.md'
Make directory creation in step 1 unconditional — remove '(если ещё не создана задачей 2)' qualifier

Clarify whether depends_on: [1] is a true file dependency or just wave ordering convention. If no files from shared/design-references/ are linked in design-retrospective SKILL.md, document the wave-ordering rationale explicitly.
